answer:
The LIFO method assumes the most recently purchased items are sold first, so the remaining inventory consists of the earliest purchases. Here, the earliest 30 units come from the December 2 inventory of 6 units at $2.30 and 24 units from the January 15 purchase at $2.50. The value is (6 × $2.30) + (24 × $2.50) = $13.80 + $60.00 = $73.80.  

\boxed{I}
